Entity_Description: Maywood is a high-density neighbourhood in south Burnaby, British Columbia, with a population of about 19,650 (2021). It lies immediately south of Metropolis at Metrotown, British Columbia's largest shopping centre, between Kingsway and Imperial Street, roughly 11 km east of downtown Vancouver. Served by the SkyTrain Expo Line at Metrotown and Patterson stations and bordered on the west by Central Park, Maywood mixes 1960s-70s low-rise walk-up apartments with new residential towers rising as Burnaby develops Metrotown into its downtown.

Squeezed between Kingsway and Imperial Street just south of Metropolis at Metrotown, this Burnaby neighbourhood of roughly 19,650 people is trading its 1960s walk-up apartments for some of the tallest residential towers in the city. That mix — aging low-rise rentals beside brand-new strata high-rises — makes {SERVICE} here anything but one-size-fits-all. {COMPANY_NAME} is a {BUSINESS_TYPE} that works both ends of that spectrum, from postwar walk-ups near the Bonsor Recreation Complex to new towers a block from the Metrotown SkyTrain station. Whether you manage a strata, rent, or own, you get {UNIQUE_VALUE_PROP} backed by {YEARS_IN_BUSINESS} years of experience. Ready for straightforward answers and a {ESTIMATE_TYPE}? Call {PHONE}.
Long_Description:
Maywood occupies one of the most intensely urban square kilometres in British Columbia. Bounded roughly by Kingsway to the north and Imperial Street to the south, the neighbourhood sits directly below Metropolis at Metrotown — the province's largest shopping centre — and packs about 19,650 residents into a tight grid of apartment streets served by the Metrotown and Patterson SkyTrain stations on the Expo Line. Central Park forms the green western edge, and the Bonsor Recreation Complex anchors community life in the middle of it all.

That density shapes what {SERVICE} looks like here. Maywood's building stock tells two stories at once: three-storey walk-up rental apartments from the 1960s and 1970s still line many blocks, while concrete high-rise towers — some of the tallest residential buildings in Burnaby — keep rising as the city develops Metrotown into its official downtown. A {BUSINESS_TYPE} working in Maywood has to move comfortably between both worlds: aging low-rise buildings carrying decades of wear, and new strata towers with councils, property managers, and formal approval processes. {COMPANY_NAME} does exactly that, bringing {SERVICE_DESCRIPTION} to renters, owners, and strata corporations alike.

The Metro Vancouver climate adds its own pressure. From November through March, persistent Pacific rain soaks the neighbourhood, driving moisture, drainage, and moss and algae problems on building exteriors — a particular concern for Maywood's older walk-ups, many built long before modern rain-screen standards. Summers turn warm and dry, which is when much of the catch-up work gets done.

Economically, Maywood runs on the Metrotown engine: retail and services at Metropolis, businesses along the Kingsway corridor, and a workforce that commutes by SkyTrain across the region. Many residents rent and many buildings are professionally managed, so responsiveness and clear scheduling matter more here than almost anywhere else in Burnaby. {COMPANY_NAME} answers that with {RESPONSE_TIME} response, {UNIQUE_VALUE_PROP}, and a {RATING}-rated track record built over {YEARS_IN_BUSINESS} years.

From the tower cluster beside Metrotown Station to the quiet apartment blocks backing onto Central Park, {COMPANY_NAME} treats Maywood as core service territory — not an afterthought at the edge of a coverage map. For a {ESTIMATE_TYPE} anywhere in the neighbourhood, call {PHONE}.
Local_Facts:
- Maywood is a neighbourhood in south Burnaby, Metro Vancouver, about 11 km east of downtown Vancouver
- Population of approximately 19,650 (2021), making it one of Burnaby's densest neighbourhoods
- Sits immediately south of Metropolis at Metrotown, British Columbia's largest shopping centre
- Served by two SkyTrain Expo Line stations: Metrotown and Patterson
- Bordered on the west by Central Park; the Bonsor Recreation Complex sits within the neighbourhood
- Building stock mixes 1960s-70s low-rise walk-up apartments with new high-rise towers as Burnaby develops Metrotown into its downtown
- Mild oceanic climate with heavy November-March rainfall and warm, dry summers
